name: Circuit Graph Operations
description: Pure functions that operate on frozen CircuitSpec models and return new instances. Provides graph manipulation operations for circuit specifications including node/edge management, cycle detection, and topological sorting.

functions.name: add_node
functions.description: Add a node to the circuit. Auto-assigns port if not specified (port=0). Creates a new CircuitSpec with the added node.

inputs.name: circuit
inputs.description: The circuit to add a node to

inputs.name: name
inputs.description: Name of the node to add

inputs.name: port
inputs.description: Port number for the node. If 0, auto-assigns next available port.

error_cases.name: duplicate_node
error_cases.condition: Node with given name already exists in circuit

error_cases.name: no_available_port
error_cases.condition: port=0 and all ports in range [9001, 9999] are used

preconditions: Node with given name must not already exist in circuit
If port=0, there must be an available port in range [9001, 9999]
postconditions: Returns new CircuitSpec with added node
Original circuit is unchanged (pure function)
New circuit has same name and version as original
New circuit has all original nodes plus new node
New circuit has all original edges unchanged

functions.name: remove_node
functions.description: Remove a node and all its edges from the circuit. Returns new CircuitSpec without the specified node.

inputs.name: circuit
inputs.description: The circuit to remove a node from

inputs.name: name
inputs.description: Name of the node to remove

error_cases.name: node_not_found
error_cases.condition: Node with given name does not exist in circuit

preconditions: Node with given name must exist in circuit
postconditions: Returns new CircuitSpec without the specified node
Original circuit is unchanged (pure function)
New circuit has same name and version as original
All edges where node is source or target are removed
All other nodes and edges remain unchanged

functions.name: add_edge
functions.description: Add a directed edge between two nodes. Creates a new CircuitSpec with the added edge.

inputs.name: circuit
inputs.description: The circuit to add an edge to

error_cases.name: source_not_found
error_cases.condition: Source node does not exist in circuit

error_cases.name: target_not_found
error_cases.condition: Target node does not exist in circuit

error_cases.name: duplicate_edge
error_cases.condition: Edge from source to target already exists

preconditions: Source node must exist in circuit
Target node must exist in circuit
Edge from source to target must not already exist
postconditions: Returns new CircuitSpec with added edge
Original circuit is unchanged (pure function)
New circuit has same name and version as original
New circuit has all original nodes unchanged
New circuit has all original edges plus new edge

functions.name: remove_edge
functions.description: Remove a directed edge from the circuit. Returns new CircuitSpec without the specified edge.

inputs.name: circuit
inputs.description: The circuit to remove an edge from

error_cases.name: edge_not_found
error_cases.condition: Edge from source to target does not exist in circuit

preconditions: Edge from source to target must exist in circuit
postconditions: Returns new CircuitSpec without the specified edge
Original circuit is unchanged (pure function)
New circuit has same name and version as original
New circuit has all original nodes unchanged
All other edges remain unchanged

functions.name: set_contract
functions.description: Set the contract spec path for a node. Returns new CircuitSpec with updated node contract.

inputs.name: node_name
inputs.description: Name of the node to update

error_cases.name: node_not_found
error_cases.condition: Node with given name does not exist in circuit

preconditions: Node with given name must exist in circuit
postconditions: Returns new CircuitSpec with updated node contract
Original circuit is unchanged (pure function)
New circuit has same name and version as original
Specified node has contract field updated to contract_path
All other node fields and all other nodes remain unchanged
All edges remain unchanged

functions.name: has_cycle
functions.description: Check if the circuit's edge graph contains a cycle using depth-first search with color marking (WHITE=0, GRAY=1, BLACK=2).

inputs.name: circuit
inputs.description: The circuit to check for cycles

postconditions: Returns True if circuit contains at least one cycle
Returns False if circuit is acyclic (DAG)
Original circuit is unchanged (pure function)

functions.name: topological_sort
functions.description: Return node names in topological order (dependencies first) using Kahn's algorithm with sorted queue for deterministic ordering.
preconditions: Circuit must not contain a cycle (must be a DAG)
postconditions: Returns list of node names in topological order
For every edge (u, v), u appears before v in the result
Original circuit is unchanged (pure function)
Result is deterministic (queue is sorted at each step)

functions.name: _next_port
functions.description: Find the next available port in the range [DEFAULT_PORT_START, DEFAULT_PORT_MAX]. Internal helper function for port auto-assignment.

inputs.name: circuit
inputs.description: The circuit to check for used ports

error_cases.name: no_available_ports
error_cases.condition: All ports in range [9001, 9999] are used by circuit nodes

preconditions: At least one port in range [9001, 9999] must be available
postconditions: Returns the first available port in ascending order from 9001
Returned port is not used by any node in the circuit
Original circuit is unchanged (pure function)

invariants: All functions are pure (return new instances, do not mutate inputs)
All CircuitSpec modifications preserve name and version fields
Port range for auto-assignment is [9001, 9999] inclusive
